finding: _safe_destination is a structural no-op: cli_filter calls it with base derived from the destination itself (out_path.parent or input_path.parent), so is_relative_to(base) is always true and nothing is rejected, including -oc pointing outside the source dir. The RSP-6/plan-review 'path-contained' guarantee is asserted but not enforced.
proposed_action: Compute the allowed base independently of the destination; realpath each component so a symlinked ancestor cannot escape; add rejection tests.

finding: A symlinked output directory escapes the check: if out_dir or an ancestor is a symlink, both dest and base resolve through it and the check passes. Only the final component is symlink-checked.
proposed_action: Resolve/realpath ancestors and require the realpath'd parent to be inside the realpath'd base.

finding: filter reads the entire input file and sends it to an external LLM with no size or type guard (extension-agnostic). Non-interactive mode proceeds without egress confirmation, so a huge/binary/secret file could be transmitted off-box.
proposed_action: Add a configurable input size cap + require an explicit egress confirmation flag in non-interactive mode.

finding: Non-UTF-8 input raises an uncaught UnicodeDecodeError; the read_text try only catches OSError, so the user sees a Python-internal message instead of a clean 'not a text file' error.
proposed_action: Catch decode errors and raise a clean RecoveryError.

finding: Symlink checks in _safe_destination and the migration script have a check-then-use race. Negligible for a local single-user tool.
disposition: Note in comments; add a cheap re-check of not src.is_symlink() immediately before os.rename. Do NOT add FD-level locking (complexity).
column11: propose-now (cheap part); defer heavy FD hardening

finding: cli_filter calls _backup_compacted_bu(dest) and then write_text(dest,...) which itself calls _backup_if_exists(dest). Harmless but confusing double-backup path.
proposed_action: Keep exactly one backup mechanism for the filter write path.
